---
title: "Online Supplement for CRPTO: Auditing Temporal Transport and Comparator Choice in Conformal Portfolios"
author: "Anonymous"
date: "July 2026"
lang: en
bibliography: ../book/references.bib
csl: ../book/apa.csl
link-citations: true
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
    css: ijds.css
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    number-sections: false
    geometry:
      - margin=0.85in
execute:
  freeze: auto
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

# Scope and Evidence Contract

This supplement supports one retrospective CRPTO audit. It does not describe
successive paper variants or select a policy after observing outcomes. All nine
combinations of risk tolerance and conformal mixing weight are co-primary. The
audited objects are fixed score taxonomies, availability-safe residual
calibration, monthly allocations, three comparator rules, a point-cap
frontier, sharp bounds for unresolved binary outcomes, and a controlled
simulation.

The archive had been inspected in earlier project work. The current protocol
was locked before its complete run, but it is not a pristine lockbox and not a
prospective trial or preregistration. Exact repository coordinates are omitted
from anonymous-review surfaces and preserved in an editor-only crosswalk.

| Reader question | Location | Answer |
|---|---|---|
| Can every candidate exist at decision time? | Appendix A | Yes; membership is status independent |
| What receives the 90% target? | Appendix B | A binary-outcome conformal interval on candidate rows |
| Is one guardrail selected? | Appendix C | No; all nine policies are co-primary |
| What is exact? | Appendix D | Score-cap algebra and unrestricted-outcome bounds |
| Which empirical signs survive? | Appendix E | Temporal coverage failure; no portfolio sign over the finite multiverse |
| What drives the score effect? | Appendix F | Fixed stratum penalties more than endpoint clipping |
| Can the evidence be replayed? | Appendix G | Yes; frozen allocations and evaluation have explicit lineage |
| What may the paper claim? | Appendix H | Audit findings, not superiority, causality, or selected-set validity |

: Reader map for the Online Supplement. {#tbl-s-reader-map}

# Appendix A: Data and Outcome Observability

## A.1 Source and chronology

The source is the Lending Club 2007--2020Q3 research snapshot. It contains
2,925,493 rows, of which 2,060,077 have 36-month terms. Deterministic date,
schema, and origination-feature checks retain 540,121 rows across the declared
blocks. The unit of decision is an issue month, and each of the 15 primary
months receives a new USD 1 million budget.

| Block | Issue dates | Rows | Terminal defaults | Unresolved | Role |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| PD development | 2007-06--2010-12 | 17,433 | 2,377 | 0 | model fit and temporal validation |
| Probability calibration | 2011-01--2011-12 | 14,101 | 1,499 | 0 | Platt fit and taxonomy edges |
| Conformal fit | 2012-01--2012-06 | 14,967 | 2,063 | 0 | residual quantiles |
| Comparator development | 2012-07--2012-12 | 28,503 | 3,840 | 0 | outcome-free fixed comparator |
| Primary OOT | 2016-04--2017-06 | 376,890 | 57,497 | 11,551 | co-primary evaluation |
| Censored extension | 2017-07--2017-09 | 88,227 | 11,858 | 28,725 | censoring stress |

: S1 chronology and terminal-outcome observability. {#tbl-s-chronology}

## A.2 Endpoint taxonomy

Statuses containing `Charged Off` are positive and statuses containing `Fully
Paid` are negative. Exact `Default` and all nonterminal states are unresolved:
the administrative snapshot does not prove the protocol's terminal charged-off
event. Unresolved rows remain in candidate menus and are bounded only after
allocations have been frozen. The endpoint is a terminal snapshot
classification, not lifetime default risk, a hazard, or a funding response.

For label-dependent fitting, a Fully Paid label becomes observable at its last
payment date. A Charged Off label is conservatively available six months after
last payment. With the March 31, 2016 information cutoff, the retained fitting
shares are 0.997648 for PD development, 0.998298 for probability calibration,
and 0.998731 for conformal fitting.

## A.3 Physical information boundary

The decision panel contains only ID, issue month, amount, purpose, contractual
rate, calibrated PD, conformal endpoints, and fixed score group. It rejects
status, realized payoff, miscoverage, or any outcome-derived field. The outcome
panel is keyed separately. A validated one-to-one join occurs after every
allocation is persisted; missing or duplicate IDs fail the run. These checks
make leakage auditable but do not create exchangeability.

# Appendix B: Prediction and Conformal Construction

## B.1 Calibrated PD

A CatBoost model uses 29 numeric and 9 categorical origination-time features.
Its fixed settings are 500 trees, depth 6, learning rate 0.04, class balancing,
Bernoulli subsampling, and temporal ordering. The final 20% of development
months form a validation tail. A logistic Platt map is fitted on 2011 margins.
Seeds 40--44 are a declared sensitivity; no OOT metric chooses among them.

## B.2 Availability-safe split Mondrian interval

For calibrated score $p_i$ and binary endpoint $Y_i$, the residual is
$s_i=|Y_i-p_i|$. Group boundaries are quantiles of all 2011 calibrated scores;
they do not use 2012H1 labels. Within a frozen group $g$ with $n_g$ observable
labels, the residual radius is the order statistic at

$$
k_g=\left\lceil(n_g+1)(1-0.10)\right\rceil.
$$

The future interval is

$$
[\ell_i,u_i]=[\max(0,p_i-c_{g(i)}),\min(1,p_i+c_{g(i)})].
$$

The seed-42 five-group boundaries are 0.008219, 0.050265, 0.080369,
0.111894, 0.153262, and 0.475854. Pooled, two-group, and ten-group recipes are
coarsening diagnostics fixed by the same rule.

| Groups | Primary resolved coverage | All-candidate bound | Mean width | $u=1$ |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 1 | 0.876542 | [0.853029, 0.880326] | 0.924935 | 0.124901 |
| 2 | 0.878403 | [0.855929, 0.881820] | 0.583563 | 0.164961 |
| 5 | 0.876312 | [0.854714, 0.879647] | 0.664992 | 0.187084 |
| 10 | 0.878614 | [0.857404, 0.881942] | 0.666072 | 0.203415 |

: S2 primary coverage under score taxonomies fixed before OOT. {#tbl-s-coverage}

All four upper endpoints are below 0.90. The five-group conformal-fit coverage
is 0.900388. The empirical failure is temporal and precedes portfolio
selection; it is not a contradiction of finite-sample validity under
exchangeability.

# Appendix C: Portfolio and Comparator Protocol

## C.1 Coherent payoff

For annual contractual rate $r_i$ and loss given default $\lambda$, the
standardized realized and expected per-dollar payoffs are

$$
\pi_i(Y_i)=(1-Y_i)r_i-Y_i\lambda,
\qquad
\bar\pi_i=(1-p_i)r_i-p_i\lambda.
$$

The canonical $\lambda$ is 0.45; 0.25 and 0.65 are sensitivities. This endpoint
omits timing, amortization, prepayment, recoveries, fees, and discounting. An
undiscounted snapshot cash-yield diagnostic is secondary and is not called IRR
or investor return.

## C.2 Monthly program and closed policy family

Each policy maximizes $\sum_i a_i\bar\pi_i$ subject to a full USD 1 million
monthly budget, loan amount bounds, a weighted risk-score cap, and a purpose
concentration cap. The guardrail score is
$q_i=(1-\gamma)p_i+\gamma u_i$.

| Policy | $\tau$ | $\gamma$ |
|---|---:|---:|
| linear-001 | 0.15 | 0.25 |
| linear-002 | 0.15 | 0.50 |
| linear-003 | 0.15 | 0.75 |
| linear-004 | 0.17 | 0.25 |
| linear-005 | 0.17 | 0.50 |
| linear-006 | 0.17 | 0.75 |
| linear-007 | 0.19 | 0.25 |
| linear-008 | 0.19 | 0.50 |
| linear-009 | 0.19 | 0.75 |

: S3 complete co-primary policy family. IDs are labels, not ranks. {#tbl-s-policy-grid}

## C.3 Comparator rules

`C0` copies the guardrail's numeric cap onto point PD. `C1` fixes, before OOT,
the guardrail's mean funded point-PD moment over six 2012H2 monthly allocations.
`C2` freezes a guardrail allocation, computes its monthly funded point-PD
moment, and uses that value as the point-PD cap on the identical menu. C2
matches 135 canonical policy-month moments with maximum absolute residual
$4.17\times10^{-17}$; it uses no outcomes but is an audit comparator rather
than a deployable fixed rule.

The finite multiverse contains C0, C1, C2, and 29 point caps from 0.05 to 0.12
in increments of 0.0025. Canonical settings are seed 42, purpose cap 0.25, and
LGD 0.45. Sensitivities cross five seeds, purpose caps 0.20, 0.25, 0.30, and
1.00, three LGDs, and four taxonomies. Every cell is retained.

# Appendix D: Exact Statements and Bounds

## D.1 Positive affine score equivalence

If $s_i=ap_i+b$, $a>0$, and the budget binds at $B$, then

$$
\sum_i a_i s_i\le \tau_sB
\Longleftrightarrow
\sum_i a_i p_i\le ((\tau_s-b)/a)B.
$$

The proof substitutes the affine score and uses $\sum_i a_i=B$. A pooled
affine placebo reproduces the translated point-PD allocation to solver
tolerance. A stratum-specific affine score does not generally admit one global
cap translation.

## D.2 Same-cap nesting

Because $u_i\ge p_i$ and $\gamma\in[0,1]$, $q_i\ge p_i$. Hence every
guardrail-feasible allocation at cap $\tau$ is point-PD feasible at the same
cap. For the common maximization objective, the C0 point optimum cannot be
worse in expected objective. This is a positive control, not a neutral
comparison of uncertainty methods.

## D.3 Binary miscoverage

For $Y_i\in\{0,1\}$,

$$
m_i=\mathbf 1\{Y_i=0,\ell_i>0\}+
\mathbf 1\{Y_i=1,u_i<1\}.
$$

The two terms are disjoint and follow by enumerating $Y=0$ and $Y=1$.
Endpoint saturation can therefore raise coverage while adding little ranking
information.

## D.4 Sharp unrestricted-outcome bounds

For a fixed allocation and additive metric
$T(Y)=C+\sum_{i\in U}g_i(Y_i)$ over unresolved rows $U$, the bounds

$$
T_L=C+\sum_{i\in U}\min_{y\in\{0,1\}}g_i(y),\qquad
T_U=C+\sum_{i\in U}\max_{y\in\{0,1\}}g_i(y)
$$

are sharp because joint endpoint assignments attain them. Paired policy bounds
use the union of funded IDs and optimize each shared unresolved outcome once;
subtracting marginal intervals would generally be wider and not sharp.

For a finite comparator set $\mathcal J$, the envelope
$[\min_jL_j,\max_jU_j]$ has an invariant sign exactly when it lies strictly on
one side of zero. This identifies signs over the declared set only.

# Appendix E: Empirical Comparator Audit

## E.1 Canonical direction census

| Comparator | Payoff $-$ / $+$ / ? | Default $-$ / $+$ / ? | Miscoverage $-$ / $+$ / ? |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| C0 same numeric cap | 0 / 9 / 0 | 9 / 0 / 0 | 8 / 0 / 1 |
| C1 fixed development cap | 5 / 2 / 2 | 2 / 2 / 5 | 1 / 7 / 1 |
| C2 contemporaneous match | 7 / 0 / 2 | 0 / 1 / 8 | 0 / 8 / 1 |
| Finite multiverse envelope | 0 / 0 / 9 | 0 / 0 / 9 | 0 / 0 / 9 |

: S4 guardrail-minus-point direction counts. `?` means the sharp interval contains zero. {#tbl-s-direction-census}

C0 makes every guardrail appear to improve payoff and default because it is a
weaker feasible set. Under exact C2 matching, payoff is lower for 7 of 9
guardrails, default is higher for 1 of 9, and miscoverage is higher for 8 of 9;
the other intervals cross zero. No direction is universal. Across C0,
C1, C2, and the point-cap frontier, all 27 policy-by-metric envelopes contain
zero.

## E.2 Canonical C2 sharp contrasts

| Policy | Payoff bound ($) | Default bound | Miscoverage bound |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| linear-001 | [-7,380, 89,594] | [-0.009346, 0.000347] | [-0.001730, 0.006762] |
| linear-002 | [-170,958, -23,356] | [-0.001677, 0.013084] | [0.007470, 0.020645] |
| linear-003 | [-195,812, -26,691] | [-0.002158, 0.015087] | [0.012104, 0.027095] |
| linear-004 | [-45,995, 7,034] | [-0.001518, 0.003816] | [0.001487, 0.005621] |
| linear-005 | [-135,905, -6,936] | [-0.003170, 0.009673] | [0.006988, 0.018245] |
| linear-006 | [-182,723, -30,427] | [-0.001139, 0.014268] | [0.011468, 0.025099] |
| linear-007 | [-43,303, -3,211] | [-0.000845, 0.003008] | [0.000033, 0.003102] |
| linear-008 | [-157,070, -25,322] | [-0.000519, 0.012567] | [0.007330, 0.018829] |
| linear-009 | [-218,919, -74,358] | [0.002888, 0.017311] | [0.013610, 0.026446] |

: S5 canonical C2 paired sharp bounds, seed 42, purpose cap 0.25, LGD 0.45. {#tbl-s-c2-bounds}

These are retrospective fixed-policy differences, not treatment effects. The
only policy with a sign-robust default increase is linear-009. It is not
promoted as a representative winner or loser.

## E.3 Point-cap frontier

At point caps 0.05--0.065, at least eight guardrails have lower payoff, while
six to nine have higher default and all nine have higher miscoverage. At cap
0.0825, all nine have higher payoff and lower default, and eight have lower
miscoverage. At caps 0.085--0.095, all nine directions are favorable on all
three metrics except one payoff interval at 0.0875 and 0.09. The frontier then
becomes less uniform at selected higher caps. This pattern is why one cap
cannot stand in for comparator identification.

## E.4 Seeds and purpose caps

Purpose caps 0.20, 0.25, and 0.30 bind in every guardrail-month, seed, and
policy; cap 1.00 removes that constraint. Across 180 C2 cells:

| Metric | Negative | Positive | Indeterminate |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Standardized payoff | 59 | 37 | 84 |
| Weighted default | 50 | 33 | 97 |
| Weighted miscoverage | 33 | 64 | 83 |

: S6 complete C2 seed-purpose census. {#tbl-s-seed-cap}

The full machine-readable table reports every seed-policy-cap cell. Mixed signs
show interaction between score perturbations and binding operational
constraints, not Monte Carlo uncertainty around one selected policy.

## E.5 LGD and cash endpoint

Reoptimization at LGD 0.25, 0.45, and 0.65 changes multiple payoff and default
directions. The canonical LGD is a declared normalization, not an estimated
recovery model. The secondary snapshot cash-yield diagnostic agrees with the
canonical C2 payoff direction for six policies and differs for three. Economic
claims are therefore restricted to the explicitly defined endpoint.

# Appendix F: Mechanism and Controlled Simulation

## F.1 Frozen-score ablations

The five-group score contains fixed stratum penalties and clipping at one.
Removing clipping while retaining the penalties makes comparatively small
allocation changes. Replacing group penalties with one pooled affine penalty
changes USD 4,700--46,600 of the USD 15 million canonical allocation, depending on
policy. The pooled score with its translated cap reproduces point PD exactly.
The material channel is the interaction of stratum-specific penalties with
binding constraints, not the conformal label itself.

## F.2 Simulation design and result

At each temporal shift in $\{0,0.05,0.10,0.15\}$, 200 repetitions independently
generate calibration and evaluation samples, fit the same binary residual
construction, and solve same-cap and exactly matched point comparators.

| Shift | Calibration coverage | Transported coverage | Saturation | Taxonomy allocation $L_1$ | Guardrail $-$ C0 default | Guardrail $-$ C2 default |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 0.00 | 0.903340 | 0.901618 | 0.094375 | 0.052318 | -0.069322 | -0.002634 |
| 0.05 | 0.903375 | 0.898707 | 0.105928 | 0.056200 | -0.072641 | 0.001092 |
| 0.10 | 0.903385 | 0.895058 | 0.112745 | 0.055684 | -0.076036 | 0.000132 |
| 0.15 | 0.903380 | 0.892312 | 0.123423 | 0.057688 | -0.081602 | -0.002178 |

: S7 simulation means over 200 repetitions per shift. {#tbl-s-simulation}

The 5--95% intervals for guardrail-minus-C2 default contain zero at every
shift. Clipping-only allocation distance remains below 0.002 on average, while
taxonomy distance remains 0.052--0.058. The simulation is a mechanism check,
not a calibrated model of Lending Club.

# Appendix G: Reproducibility and Lineage

## G.1 Two-phase execution

The first locked execution completed every outcome-free operation: 493,620
decision rows, five fitted models, four taxonomies, 7,347 solve records, and
718,925 funded allocation rows. It wrote a cryptographic freeze before any
outcome join. Its original evaluator repeatedly merged the full outcome panel
and did not finish within the execution window.

The recovery execution validates the source protocol, model and recipe
descriptors, row cardinalities, and allocation hashes, then reuses only frozen
outcome-free objects. One vectorized join produces 7,347 monthly evaluations,
515 aggregate rows, 504 paired contrasts, 594 coverage rows, and 800 simulation
repetitions. It changes evaluation complexity, not scores or allocations.

## G.2 Evidence build and gates

The evidence builder rejects a mismatched protocol, incomplete budget,
unexpected solver, failed C2 match, missing receipt, or changed source hash. It
generates 41 table and figure files plus one manifest. Consecutive builds are
byte-identical. The review pipeline then checks claims across the QMD body,
supplement, official IJDS TeX, and generated evidence; runs unit, typing, lint,
champion-integrity, and numerical-drift gates; compiles PDFs; and inspects their
rendered pages.

The protected historical champion and extraction manifest are separate legacy
objects. Neither is executed or overwritten by this audit.

## G.3 Anonymous review boundary

Run names, commits, tags, hashes, remote URLs, and machine paths are absent from
reviewer-facing files because they can reveal authorship. An editor-only
crosswalk maps the anonymous descriptions to immutable artifacts. On
acceptance, the code, environment lock, evidence manifest, content-addressed
data pointers, and raw reconstruction instructions can be released.

# Appendix H: Claim Boundary and Limitations

| Evidence | Supported statement | Not supported |
|---|---|---|
| Four fixed taxonomies | OOT candidate coverage upper bounds are below 0.90 | General conformal invalidity |
| C0 nesting | Same numeric cap is a weaker point-PD constraint | Neutral uncertainty comparison |
| Exact C2 matching | One funded point-PD moment is equal | Equal feasible sets or unique counterfactual |
| Sharp paired bounds | Fixed-policy signs under unrestricted unresolved labels | Sampling confidence or missing-at-random identification |
| 27 finite envelopes | No sign is invariant over the declared comparators | Invariance over every possible comparator |
| 180 seed-cap cells | Operational choices interact with policy direction | A selected best seed, cap, or guardrail |
| Simulation | Drift and comparator mechanics can reproduce the pattern | Structural model of the lending market |

: S8 active claim boundary. {#tbl-s-claims}

The remaining limitations are substantive. The archive is one discontinued
retail platform; terminal status is not a survival outcome; snapshot payoff is
not discounted return; temporal exchangeability fails; funded-set coverage is
not guaranteed; C2 is an audit comparator; continuous allocation and purpose
caps simplify operations; and earlier archive inspection prevents a pristine
confirmatory interpretation. The paper's defensible result is correspondingly
narrow: temporal coverage failure is robust here, while portfolio direction is
not invariant to comparator stringency or binding operational constraints.
